name: sbuf_unique_implies_no_duplicate_contains
code:
proof fn phi_3_sbuf_unique_implies_no_duplicate_contains(state: State, i: int, j: int)
    requires
        state.writer_sbuf_entries_are_unique(),
        0 <= i < state.writer_sbuf().len(),
        0 <= j < state.writer_sbuf().len(),
        state.writer_sbuf()[i].0 == state.writer_sbuf()[j].0,
    ensures
        i == j,
{
}
reason: Address uniqueness in the writer sbuf should mean each address appears exactly once — tests that the forall quantifier correctly captures injectivity

proof fn phi_4_valid_core_external_body_no_constraints(c1: Constants, c2: Constants, core: Core)
    requires
        c1.valid_core(core),
        c1.node_count == c2.node_count,
        c1.core_count == c2.core_count,
    ensures
        c2.valid_core(core),
{
}
reason: `valid_core` is `external_body` — it may depend on fields beyond `node_count`/`core_count` (like range_ptmem), but being opaque means Verus can't verify this relationship

name: history_pending_maps_unmaps_independent
code:
proof fn phi_5_history_pending_maps_unmaps_independent(h: History, va: usize, pte: PTE)
    requires
        h.pending_maps.contains_key(va),
    ensures
        h.pending_maps.contains_key(va) || h.pending_unmaps.contains_key(va),
{
}
reason: `pending_maps` and `pending_unmaps` are independent Map fields with no mutual exclusion constraint — a vaddr could be in both maps simultaneously, which would be inconsistent
